meta.title: Service & Support | Engineering Delivery and After-Sales | WEI LAN
meta.description: Learn how WEI LAN supports overseas recycling projects with pre-sales consulting, process planning, manufacturing quality control, commissioning, training, spare parts, and remote support.

pageTitle: Support from proposal review to stable operation
bodyHtml:
Project Support

Project support runs from technical review to stable operation

Overseas project teams evaluate more than equipment. They also need to know whether the supplier can support process definition, layout planning, commissioning, training, and follow-up operations after startup.

Technical Review Delivery Planning Commissioning Remote Support

Support Scope

Service delivery is structured from technical review to operating support

The support model is designed to reduce uncertainty for project owners, EPC contractors, and recycling operators who need technical clarity before committing to equipment configuration.

01

Front-end technical review

Share waste type, composition, target output, capacity expectation, and local conditions so WEI LAN can recommend a suitable system direction early.

02

Material analysis and process definition

Photos, videos, contamination level, required purity, target throughput, and output value path help define the practical process route.

03

Layout and configuration engineering

Capacity target, available footprint, voltage conditions, and automation level can all be reflected in the proposed layout.

04

Manufacturing quality control

ISO-backed quality management helps keep production control, equipment assembly, and delivery consistency visible during supplier review.

05

Packaging and shipment planning

Modular equipment structure supports transport planning, container loading strategy, and cleaner handoff for overseas sites.

06

Startup and commissioning support

Factory-prefabricated modules can simplify installation sequencing, system startup, and process tuning at project handover.

07

Operator training support

Operating guidance can cover equipment controls, routine maintenance, alarms, process adjustments, and safe daily use.

08

Spare parts and maintenance planning

Recommended spare parts, maintenance routines, inspection points, and replacement planning help reduce downtime after startup.

09

Remote support

Digital monitoring, alarm review, operating data, and remote troubleshooting help local teams keep the line stable after handover.

10

Project detail intake

Project teams can start with material photos or videos, capacity target, site limits, and project information before a full RFQ package is ready.

Service Stages

Delivery system connects pre-sales review, shipment, startup, and after-sales support

The support path separates early technical review, delivery preparation, and post-startup support so overseas teams can see what information is needed at each stage.

01

Pre-sales engineering

Start the discussion before final specifications are fixed. WEI LAN can review project information and give early direction for system scope.

  • Material composition basis
  • Material photos or videos
  • Capacity target and output target
  • Project stage and overseas site limits
02

Delivery preparation

Turn the selected equipment path into a practical delivery plan that covers configuration, quality control, shipment, and handover work.

  • Layout and configuration engineering
  • Manufacturing quality control
  • Packaging and shipment coordination
  • Startup and commissioning support
03

After-sales operation

Support the operating team after startup with training, maintenance planning, spare parts coordination, and remote support.

  • Operator training support
  • Spare parts and maintenance planning
  • Remote support and troubleshooting
  • Operating feedback for process adjustment

Configuration Method

Configuration quality is shaped by material records, site limits, and engineering inputs

WEI LAN can review material photos or videos, material description, overseas site limits, and project information first, then align recommended capacity, process modules, and automation level with the project stage.

Material photos or videos Waste composition Capacity basis Site footprint Overseas site limits Voltage conditions Output target Automation level Project information Commissioning scope Spare parts planning

Delivery Assurance

Delivery assurance is built through engineering clarity, controlled handoff, uptime support, and approval records

For cross-border projects, delivery clarity is part of product value. The support plan shows how equipment selection turns into a practical installation and operating routine.

01

Engineering clarity

Better front-end project information leads to more realistic configuration guidance and fewer surprises during proposal review.

02

Controlled handoff

Commissioning, documentation, and operator guidance make it easier for local teams to move from equipment arrival to operation.

03

Uptime support

Remote diagnostics and spare parts planning help support long-running operation after startup.

04

Approval records

Procurement and management teams can evaluate not only the machine, but also the delivery confidence behind it.

Start an Inquiry

Configuration guidance begins with project information and documentation needs

Tell WEI LAN what waste you need to process, your capacity target, site limits, and documentation expectations. That is usually enough to start a more precise configuration review.

Request Engineering Proposal Review Solution Paths
